_test: 2C: Container safe mode trigger — healthy-grp works, broken-grp enters safe mode
_checklist: Stage 11 or 12 (one group healthy, one in safe mode)
broken-grp: safe mode marker at /var/lib/init-status/safe-mode-broken-grp OR unhealthy-broken-grp
Manual: docker exec openclaw-healthy-grp kill 1 → Docker restart: on-failure brings it back
Manual: time docker compose -p openclaw-broken-grp down → should be <10s
_instructions: OpenClaw auto-failover means a broken model name alone won't trigger safe mode.
To force broken-grp to fail: in the iOS Shortcut, set ALL THREE LLM API keys to broken values
healthy-grp will also fail E2E (same keys), but that's expected for this test.
Alternative: deploy normally, then SSH in and manually break broken-grp's group.env + restart.
_manual_break_steps: 4. Restart: sudo docker compose -f /home/bot/.openclaw/compose/broken-grp/docker-compose.yaml -p openclaw-broken-grp down && sudo docker compose -f /home/bot/.openclaw/compose/broken-grp/docker-compose.yaml -p openclaw-broken-grp up -d
5. Wait for E2E check to fail → safe mode triggers for broken-grp
name: Test-2C-ContainerSafeMode
description: Pre-merge test: container safe mode. Deploy healthy, then manually break one group's API keys to trigger safe mode.
